questions.question: The following sentence has been split into segments. One of them may contain an error. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer.Among you and tina / she is quite rude / to the boy.
questions.explanation: the error is in Option D.Among is used with more than two people or things – players chatting among themselves. The correct sentence is “Between you and tina she is quite rude to the boy.”

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice.Fake promises were being made by each coach.
options: Each coach has made fake promises.
Each coach has been making fake promises.
Each coach was making fake promises.
questions.explanation: Active voice ![](https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/website/bulk_uploader_images/1742023649876_image001.png) passive voice Subject ![](https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/website/bulk_uploader_images/1742023649876_image002.png) is/ am /are verb (Ing) object object is/ am/ are being V3 by subject. Each coach was making fake promises Fake promises were being made by each coach.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.Fight shy of
questions.explanation: The correct answer is confronting someone.Fight shy of = To try to avoid something.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Anodine”.Alternate: occur in turn repeatedly. Ascetic: avoiding physical pleasures and living a simple life. Allergic: having strong dislike or aversion. Anodyne: A medicine that relieves or allays pain.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Shall / I go / for walking / in the playground?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “for walking”.Shall is used with first person ‘I’ and ‘we’ in future tense. All the other persons take ‘will’. Instead of “for walking” we need to use “for walk”. According to rule: with preposition, we always use a noun/gerund/noun phrase. Walking is a present participle. We need to use noun form of “walking”. Correct sentence: shall I go for walk in the playground.

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer.Shivaan should be excited / on the promotion / he’s got.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “on the promotion”.Preposition “on” usually used for something specific, like a location. So, “on” cannot be used here. Here, about is used which usually means the subject of something or something less specific. The correct sentence: Shivaan should be excited on the promotion he’s got.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech.He said to me, “Do your father own this beautiful bungalow?”
options: He asked to me if your father own that beautiful bungalow.
He asked me did my father own that beautiful bungalow.
He asked me that if your father owned this beautiful bungalow.
He asked me if my father owned that beautiful bungalow.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “He asked me if my father owned that beautiful bungalow.Said to change into asked. Comma changed into if. Do your father own this beautiful bungalow change into my father owned that beautiful bungalow.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Trumpet
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option “Conceal”.Trumpet: proclaim widely or loudly. Evince: to show or express clearly or to make plain. Announce: to notify. Conceal: not allow to be seen, hide. Clarion: loud and clear.

questions.question: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives their correct order.A. Expert mechanics labored over the plane for three hours. B. A small nut had fallen into a channel which prevented the gear from moving. C. A huge plane was forced to crash land when its landing gear got stuck. D. They were amazed when they discovered what had caused the mechanical failure.
questions.explanation: the correct answer is CADB.C. A huge plane was forced to crash land when its landing gear got stuck. A. Expert mechanics labored over the plane for three hours. D. They were amazed when they discovered what had caused the mechanical failure. B. A small nut had fallen into a channel which prevented the gear from moving.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.Pallid
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “strong”.Pallid: lacking colour or brightness. Colourless: weak in colour; not colourful. Vivid: having striking color/having the highest saturation. Feeble: weak and without energy, strength, or power. Amortized: gradually write off the initial cost of (an asset) over a period. Thus, the most appropriate synonym of the given word ‘Pallid’ is ‘colourless’.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.Beguile
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Deceive. The word "beguile" means to charm or enchant someone, often in a deceptive way. The most appropriate synonym among the given options is (c) Deceive, as it aligns with the idea of using charm to mislead or trick someone. Now, let me explain all the options: Other Options: (a) Repulse: Means to cause someone to feel intense distaste or disgust. This is the opposite of "beguile," which involves attracting or charming someone. (b) Hasten: Refers to doing something quickly or urging someone to move faster. This is unrelated to the concept of charming or deceiving. (d) Disenchant: Means to cause someone to lose their charm or idealized belief in something. It contrasts with "beguile," as it describes a loss of charm rather than the act of enchanting.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.Someone who sells goods informally in public places.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Hawker. Hawker: Someone who sells goods informally in public places. Vendor: A person or company offering something for sale, especially a trader in the street. Florist: A person who sells and arranges cut flowers. Dealer: A person who buys and sells goods.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.Can Seema arrange for the consignment ______ delivered on Wednesday?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “to be”. In interrogative sentence we use the verb “to be”. So, the most appropriate option for the given blank is “to be”. Complete sentence: Can you arrange for the consignment to be delivered on Wednesday.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the bold part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement'.Karina had to undergo great many difficulties.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “a great many difficulties”.Use “a great many ![](https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/website/bulk_uploader_images/1742023649876_image002.png) plural noun” which means a large number of something. The correct sentence: Karina had to undergo a great many difficulties.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Cultivate
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Crude.Cultivate: try to acquire or develop (a quality or skill). Refined: developed or improved so as to be precise or subtle. Crude: Simple and not skillfully done or made. Genteel: refined.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.He is looking after the new born baby with great affection.
options: The new born baby is looked after with great affection.
The new born baby were looked after with great affection.
He is looked after with great affection by the new born baby.
The new born baby is being looked after with great affection.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is D. Active voice ![](https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/website/bulk_uploader_images/1742023649876_image001.png) passive voice Subject + is/ am/ are + verb (ing) +object object + is/ am/ are + being + V3 + by + subject He is looking after the new born baby with great affection The new born baby is being looked after with great affection

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the bold part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement'.Pushpa is so far the bright student in the college.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C.There is an error in adjective we need to use the superlative adjective (brightest). Hence, the correct improvement of the underlined par is by far the brightest. So, option {c} is the correct answer. Correct sentence: you are by far the brightest student in the class.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.Greener pastures
options: A better or more exciting job or place
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A.Greener pastures: A better or more promising situation.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.Prince does not / like to read / these kind / of novels.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option A.“Kind of novels” is a singular phrase. So, to make the sentence correct we need to use this instead of these. The correct sentence: Prince do not like to read this kind of novels.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.The management of relationships between countries
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option d.Diplomacy: the management of relationships between countries Apartheid: Discrimination based on the colour of skin and race.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.Air India's sale to Tata Group was announced on 8 October last year and a Letter of Intent (LoI) was (1) ______ to the Tata Group confirming the government's willingness to sell (2) _______100% stake in the airline. On 25 October, the Centre (3) ______ the share purchase agreement (SPA) for this deal. The Indian government handed (4) ______ Air India to the Tata Group on 27 January, nearly 69 years after it was taken from the conglomerate. (5) ______, Ratan Tata shared his views on Twitter stating 'Welcome Back, Air India'. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no.1.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option d “issued”.Since the sentence is in the past tense, we need to use the past tense form of the verb “to issue,” which is “issued”. Therefore, option d is the most appropriate answer.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.Air India's sale to Tata Group was announced on 8 October last year and a Letter of Intent (LoI) was (1) ______ to the Tata Group confirming the government's willingness to sell (2) _______100% stake in the airline. On 25 October, the Centre (3) ______ the share purchase agreement (SPA) for this deal. The Indian government handed (4) ______ Air India to the Tata Group on 27 January, nearly 69 years after it was taken from the conglomerate. (5) ______, Ratan Tata shared his views on Twitter stating 'Welcome Back, Air India'. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no.2.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option {c} “its”.In the given sentence, the government’s willingness to sell a 100% stake in the airline is being confirmed. Since the subject is the government, the possessive pronoun that refers to the government should be used. The pronoun “its” is the correct possessive pronoun for referring to the government. Therefore, option {c} is the most appropriate answer.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.Air India's sale to Tata Group was announced on 8 October last year and a Letter of Intent (LoI) was (1) ______ to the Tata Group confirming the government's willingness to sell (2) _______100% stake in the airline. On 25 October, the Centre (3) ______ the share purchase agreement (SPA) for this deal. The Indian government handed (4) ______ Air India to the Tata Group on 27 January, nearly 69 years after it was taken from the conglomerate. (5) ______, Ratan Tata shared his views on Twitter stating 'Welcome Back, Air India'. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no.3.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option {a} “signed”.The sentence talks about the Centre’s action regarding the share purchase agreement (SPA) for the deal between Air India and the Tata Group. The verb that best fits in this context is “signed.” The Centre signed the share purchase agreement, indicating their official approval and endorsement of the deal. Therefore, option {a} is the most appropriate answer.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.Air India's sale to Tata Group was announced on 8 October last year and a Letter of Intent (LoI) was (1) ______ to the Tata Group confirming the government's willingness to sell (2) _______100% stake in the airline. On 25 October, the Centre (3) ______ the share purchase agreement (SPA) for this deal. The Indian government handed (4) ______ Air India to the Tata Group on 27 January, nearly 69 years after it was taken from the conglomerate. (5) ______, Ratan Tata shared his views on Twitter stating 'Welcome Back, Air India'. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no.4.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option {d} “over”.The preposition ‘over’ is used to refer to who or what someone is controlled or influenced by someone or something.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.Air India's sale to Tata Group was announced on 8 October last year and a Letter of Intent (LoI) was (1) ______ to the Tata Group confirming the government's willingness to sell (2) _______100% stake in the airline. On 25 October, the Centre (3) ______ the share purchase agreement (SPA) for this deal. The Indian government handed (4) ______ Air India to the Tata Group on 27 January, nearly 69 years after it was taken from the conglomerate. (5) ______, Ratan Tata shared his views on Twitter stating 'Welcome Back, Air India'. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no.5.
questions.explanation: Option c is the correct answer. The word Joyous suits the best which means full of happiness and joy. Merry: characterized by festivity and enjoyment. Lively: Full of activity and excitement. Wonderful: Inspiring delight, pleasure, or admiration; extremely good. The completer passage is: Air India's sale to Tata Group was announced on 8 October last year and a Letter of Intent (LoI) was 1. Issued to the Tata Group confirming the government's willingness to sell 2. Its 100% stake in the airline. On 25 October, the Centre 3. Signed the share purchase agreement (SPA) for this deal. The Indian government handed 4. Over Air India to the Tata Group on 27 January, nearly 69 years after it was taken from the conglomerate. 5. Joyous, Ratan Tata shared his views on Twitter stating 'Welcome Back, Air India'.
